entry.title: How to use MAN_CALL to send other document than MSDS
entry.content:
Hello there,

We want to use Man-Call function to ship a document which is not an SDS document (in fact it is Certified Product Data Sheet for Canada), but when doing this we are having (of course) same email content as for SDS when it is sent to customers as we are using same report category (MSDS) and same business process (MSDS_DIST).

Is there a simple way to send this document through CVD1 / Man-Call with a different email content than for MSDS(s)? Does this need Abap development?
Thanks a lot for your help

Daman

entry.title: Re: Where can I find free material to study SAP SuccessFa...
entry.content:
To learn SAP SuccessFactors Compensation, you can follow a structured approach to maximize your understanding and practical knowledge. Here’s a step-by-step guide to help you get started:

  1. Implementation Guide: Begin by reviewing the comprehensive SAP SuccessFactors Compensation Implementation Guide. This document is quite detailed, spanning approximately 450 pages, and offers in-depth insights into the implementation process. You can access it directly through this link: SAP SuccessFactors Compensation Implementation Guide. Make sure to read through the various sections to grasp the intricacies of compensation management within SuccessFactors.

  2. Overview Blog: For a more digestible introduction, check out this overview blog that provides a summary of SAP SuccessFactors Compensation. It's a great starting point to get familiar with the key concepts and benefits of the system: Introduction & Overview - Part 1.

  3. Implementation Essentials: To understand the core requirements and steps involved in implementing SuccessFactors Compensation, refer to this detailed blog post on implementation essentials. It outlines the necessary preparation and requirement-gathering processes: Implementation Requirement Gathering Steps.

  4. Supplementary Learning: If you find yourself needing a break from reading or prefer a different learning format, consider watching videos related to SAP SuccessFactors Compensation. ZaranTech offers valuable video content on compensation implementation that might provide additional insights and practical examples.

  5. Also explore SAP Learning there are amazing free courses on compensation.

By following these resources, you’ll build a solid foundation in SAP SuccessFactors Compensation and be well-prepared for implementation and administration tasks.

entry.title: Re: Task 2 - CDS View Entity Associations ( August Developer Challenge )
entry.content: Yes, the warning comes up as there is a potential for the data to be altered like example if it has translations. To avoid this, you can filter out by specifying the language and add '1' to document the change cardinality. Check this link for more information: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/acquire-core-abap-skills/using-associations-in-path-expressions_daff758b-a544-4613-9a6e-aa1a2df73a33

entry.title: Re: Deploy Typescript-SAPUI5 Application to BTP
entry.content:
Hi,
for the people who face a similar issue. I was able to solve the described one by adding missing tasks to the ui5.yaml, so that the customTasks-section looks like this:

 

customTasks:
  - name: ui5-tooling-modules-task
    afterTask: replaceVersion
    configuration:
      addToNamespace: true
  - name: ui5-tooling-transpile-task
    afterTask: replaceVersion
  - name: ui5-task-zipper
    afterTask: generateCachebusterInfo
    configuration:
      archiveName: <your_projectname>
      additionalFiles:
      - xs-app.json

 

Best regards,
Lukas

entry.title: Re: Integrating Successfactors Recruiting with customer c...
entry.content:
Hi Alfonso,

SAP does not provide any documentation regarding custom integrations as we do not support them. It is that SAP SuccessFactors provides APIv2 (or v4) framework that allows building custom integrations, ref. to OData V2 HXM Reference Guide . There is nothing for scraping websites, at least I am not aware of such SAP tool - you need to search on the market.

Regards, Piotr

entry.title: Re: Is SAP and Sustainability a Myth ?
entry.content:
Hello @smarchesini 

Thank you for your comment. I'll make sure to read the book. 

To me sustainability is the ability to operate in the long-term as we operate currently. The fact is that if anything grows 25% a year over a term of 100 years, which is hardly long term in the human civilization perspective, it needs to grow 4.9 billion times. That is a simple math. Therefore it is not sustainable. Is it not factfulness?

Hi Johan,

SAP Certified Associate - SAP Activate Project Manager verifies that you have learned the basics of SAP Activate Methodology and its different phases and the deliverables therein. As you may have already noticed, attaining this certification requires you to go through the course ACT200 (Discovering SAP Activate - Agile Project Delivery) as the topics of agilitiy is being addressed in the ceritification questions.

I would rather advise you to start with this one!

Best Regards, 

Omar

entry.title: SAP IAG Connection with Service Now and AD
entry.content:
Problem Statement - We have a requirement that Service Now or Active Directory gets integrated with SAP IAG and is able to create Access Requests in IAG automatically. We got a confirmation from our AD Team that this API Integration is not possible. We also raised an OSS with SAP and they confirmed back that Service Now Integration with IAG is possible if Service Now is SCIM compatible.

Understanding - The Service now team needs to use the standard IAG API Scenarios to communicate with IAG. But IAG cannot communicate back to Service Now. We got a confirmation that this is a simplex connection. So how can the Access Request status be again sent to Service Now.

Requirement - Please do let us know if you have done any such kind of connection between IAG, Service Now or AD. How can the IAG Access request closure status be communicated with Service Now ?

How can I change the Destination Handling unit for Picking on RF device?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to Change Destination Handling unit for Picking o...
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

You can use the exception code CHHU to change the destination handling unit (HU) in a warehouse task in the RF system. For further reference, check the documentation Exceptions.

Thank you and best regards,

Geovana

entry.title: CAP: $count=true returning 0 as the amount although the results are not empty
entry.content:
Hello CAP Experts,

I was creating a basic CAP task list application within the SAP Build Code which works out of the box. Now, I wanted to add a service that only returns the user's tasks. While the basic services/entities are returning the correct amount, my custom service is returning the correct entities in the value but the "@odata.count" is 0. 

This is what is being returned for my current user:

  "@odata.context": "$metadata#UserTasks",
  "@odata.count": 0,
  "value": [
    {
      "ID": "a82f72e9-4ef4-4bed-8aae-60995e5effc0",
      "doneDateTime": null,
      "status": "Open",
      "userMail": "xxx@x.com",
      "taskDescription": "Restock shelves"
    },
    {
      "ID": "1fb9b006-11f9-42b7-b6fc-8f6a57076e63",
      "doneDateTime": null,
      "status": "Open",
      "userMail": "xxx@x.com",
      "taskDescription": "Inventory check"
    },
    {
      "ID": "8d84f9d7-6b62-4e55-8ad1-2dc77390afcb",
      "doneDateTime": "2021-07-01T10:00:00Z",
      "status": "Done,
      "userMail": "xxx@x.com",
      "taskDescription": "Staff training"
    }
  ]
}

  As you can see, the "@odata.count" is 0 although there are multiple entries. Here is my service.cds and service.js:

@Path : '/service/taskapp'
service taskappSrv
{
    @odata.draft.enabled
    entity GenericTasks as
        projection on my.GenericTasks;

    entity UserTasks as projection on my.Tasks {
        *,
        genericTask.description as taskDescription
    };

    entity OpenTasks as 
        projection on my.Tasks
        where status = 'Open';

    @CDS.redirection.target : 'Tasks'
    entity Tasks as
        projection on my.Tasks;
}

UserTasks is the service that is being requested. Here is my custom logic:

module.exports = cds.service.impl(async function () {
  const { UserTasks } = this.entities;

  this.on('READ', 'UserTasks', async (req) => {
    const userMail = req.headers["x-username"];

    console.log('Requested userMail', userMail);

    if (!userMail) {
      req.error(400, 'Parameter "userMail" ist erforderlich');
      return;
    }

    const tasks = await SELECT.from(UserTasks)
      .columns('ID', 'doneDateTime', 'status', 'userMail', 'taskDescription')
      .where({ userMail: userMail });

    console.log('Filtered tasks with description:', tasks);

    return tasks;
  });
});

It is returning and logging the tasks correctly, however my only problem is that the "@odata.count" is 0 in the final response and I don't find any way to change it. The URL that is requested:

/service/taskapp/UserTasks?$count=true

Another thread had the same problem but as I was using the newer version, I am already using oData v4. Also, in the documentation I could not find a workaround for this.

Best regards

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere: Using Replication Flows on Finance Fo...
entry.content: I am also facing issue for finance foundation package for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA cloud in I_SEMTAGGLACCOUNT CDS view. while mapping I_SEMTAGGLACCOUNT with local table in datasphere I am facing below issue (Change target object. The target object cannot be used because it does not have the required technical column __load_package_id. You can rename the target object using a name that does not exist yet. The system then creates a new object that has the same definition as the source object and contains the technical column. Alternatively, you can map the target object to an existing object that has the required technical column (__load_package_id).

entry.title: Re: XML tags as list in Content Modifier
entry.content:
Below is the XSLT code to extract '<value>' elements from incoming message

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<xsl:stylesheet version="3.0"
	xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform">
	<xsl:template match="/">
		<xsl:for-each select="//value">
			<values>
				<xsl:value-of select="."/>
			</values>
		</xsl:for-each>
	</xsl:template>
</xsl:stylesheet>

entry.title: Re: How to set up Routing to Typed View (to use XML Templ...
entry.content:
In case somebody else encounters this problem. This is how I solved it:

In manifest.json

"targets": {
    "WithTemplating": {
        "viewName": "module:path/to/view/TypedView",
        "viewType": null,
        "viewPath": null,
        "title": "{{templateTitle}}"
    },
    "XMLView": {
        "viewName": "StandardXml",
        "title": "{{xmlTitle}}"
    }
}

In path/to/view/TypedView.ts:

import Component from "sap/ui/core/Component";
import Control from "sap/ui/core/Control";
import View from "sap/ui/core/mvc/View";
import XMLView from "sap/ui/core/mvc/XMLView";

/**
 * @namespace path.to.view
 */
export default class TypedView extends View {
    public createContent(): Control | Control[] | Promise<Control | Control[]> {
		return XMLView.create({
			preprocessors: {
				xml: {
					...
				},
			},
			viewName: "path.to.view.ViewWithTemplate",
		});
	}
}

 I hope this helps somebody 🙂

entry.title: Re: Download an internal table into excel file with extension '.xls'.
entry.content: Excel checks that the format of the file and the file extension are consistent. You can't change this Excel behavior. It has been explained in lots of Excel forums.

entry.title: Re: Two main items (A+B) with one free item(C) how can we...
entry.content: I've achieved something similar (adding order items based on existing items and quantities) with code in the program MV45AFZZ / userexit_save_document_prepare...

entry.title: Enable Dropdown List of Users for Manage Members in SAP Lobby
entry.content:
Recently, someone asked me about this message you get when you try to share a project with other users.

It wasn't clear what this was about, but then I learned: instead of entering the users email as text, you can get "value help" or "autocomplete" so you can easily find the user you want to add.

You must first create a system user in your IAS tenant ...

... and create a client ID and secret.

Finally, create a destination called Identity_Authentication_Connectivity_IDS. The URL is in this format:

 

https://<IAS tenant name>.accounts.ondemand.com/scim

 

Use BasicAuthentication, and set the user and password to the client ID and secret from IAS for the new system administrator. (The documentation also explains how to use certificates for the authentication.)

Back in SAP Build lobby, you can select Manage Members for one of the projects, and then start typing a name and you will get autocomplete. Here, I type DJ and see DJ's user, which I can select to share the project with.

You'll also notice that the message we talked about at the start is no longer there.

I did not see a way to select an IAS group. If you know how, let me know.

😺

entry.title: will pMRP consider open sales order and Production order load in Workcenter
entry.content:
Hi All,

Or will it consider the open sales orders and load due to open production orders also?

entry.title: Where can I learn how to create, plan, and execute investment projects in SAP S/4HANA EPPM Project?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Exploring Investment Projects in SAP S/4HANA learning journey.


Overview
In this Learning Journey you will learn how to create, plan and execute investment projects in SAP S/4HANA EPPM Project System. You will experience the end-to-end business process, from creation of projects structures, planning of costs and budgeting, to period-end settlement of projects.

Learning objectives

After completing this Learning Journey you will be able to:

  • Create investment projects using templates.
  • Create and maintain Work Breakdown Structures (WBS).
  • Carry out cost planning of investment projects.
  • Carry out budgeting of investment projects.
  • Release and execute investment projects.
  • Run period-end closing activities and settle projects to asset accounting.
  • Use reporting features in SAP S/4HANA to analyzes structure and costs of investment projects.

 

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you.

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Adding elements to Trainer bar
entry.content: I am trying to add the search button and dropdown table of contents to the trainer bar, but I can't seem to find where this is specified in the Playback settings. Anyone know how to add these?

entry.title: Re: Access to payment order number range for using the Pa...
entry.content:
Hello, One scope item that I could find that uses these apps is 2DP (related to contract accounting). Now you can check if this scope item is relevant for the country of the company code that you are using. Of course you need to check the roles required for this app from Fiori apps library. 

Note: In the fiori apps library, you can find the list of relevant scope items by choosing the 'List View' and selecting the column 'Scope Item' for display.

Thank you!

entry.title: Task 3 - Enhancing CDS View Entities with Annotations
entry.content:
Another Wednesday and we are back with Task 3 of the August Developer Challenge on ABAP Core Data Services. We are thrilled to see the amazing response to Task 1 and Task 2. Whoever missed it can find all the information in our blogpost August Developer Challenge.

Today for Task 3 we decided to take you all through one of the interesting and widely used feature of ABAP Core Data Services – Annotations.

Annotations allow you to add domain-specific metadata and text labels to CDS objects that goes beyond the syntax features of ABAP SQL. This means that it enhances the technical properties of CDS view entities such as adding search , capabilities or controlling access to your data. To know more about Annotations watch the SAP TechByte on Annotations or go through the CDS Annotation Help Documentation

Task 3 – In this task we will add annotations to the CDS View Entity created in Task1 and then create a Fiori app to view the results.

  1. Add annotations to display all the fields in the Fiori app by default.
  2. Add annotations to provide a search field and enable search by ‘Description’ in the filter bar.
  3. Add annotations to provide a filter on the ‘Status text’ field based on Overall Status text.
  4. Add annotations to provide a filter for the Travel ID field.
  5. Create a Service Definition
  6. Create a Service Binding to preview the Fiori app.  

Follow the instructions carefully and share with us the screenshots of the items mentioned in the ‘Validation’ section of this discussion.

Please use this separate thread to ask your questions and discuss issues.

Validation :-

Please share screenshots after performing the following :

  1. Preview the App showing all the columns, search and filter options in the CDS view entity.
  2. Search records based on some text from 'Description', any value from 'Status' and apply a filter.

We are eagerly waiting to see your submissions. Enjoy!!

entry.title: Re: SAP CPI ODATA V2 jaon data POST request Issue
entry.content: The OData V2 adapter is expecting an XML payload but is receiving JSON.

entry.title: Custom Analytical Queries / CDS - Deriviation Functions
entry.content:
Hi,

im trying to understand the concept of deriviation functions in the context of custom analytical queries and cds views. 

My Goal is to understand and create deriviation functions for specific analytical query fields

As shown below, i want to create a simple Derviation Function for the field DeriviationPlaneType

I proceeded as follows (i tried to use the deriviation for CostCenter (ZF_MYCOSTCENTER) as an template):
1. My Base CDS Entity

2. My Deriviation CDS-Entity, my goal is to only have planetypes with the value '747-400'

This CDS-Entity is released (C1)

3. My Primary Data Source, as far as i understand, i need to associate the cds-entity which contains the field with the deriviation function (ZI_PLANETYPE -> ZF_MYPLANETYPE-> DeriviationPlaneType)

4. Custom CDS-View

5. Custom Analytical Query

I've created a custom analytical query based on the beforementioned custom cds-view.

But my Deriviation Function is not showing up

Did i miss anything?

I also tried to just copy F_MYCOSTCENTER

and creating a simple Cube

But i am getting an error message when using this cube in a custom analytica query when selecting my deriviation function.

I hope anyone can help me to fix this and to get an better understanding how deriviation function work.

best regards

Jonas

entry.title: System not reflecting in System monitoring
entry.content:
We are recently facing issue in our FRUN where systems integrated do not reflect in System monitoring though the SSI completed. 

This issue resolved using the Snote : 3419413 - Systems are not showing in System Monitoring UI in FRUN launchpad - SAP for Me using the resolution B by updating the report RLMDB_INDEX_UPDATE_TS 

Since we use automation to integrate the system to FRUN this affects timeline for system build and Handover activities. 

We would like a way that it accepts multiple entries currently it can update one entry at time as it is time consuming 

Thank you !

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: System not reflecting in System monitoring
entry.content:
If the SEARCH INDEX consumer is not triggered you should implement the SAP Note:

3444702 - Report to trigger LMDB notifications

And run the report with consumer id CL_LMDB_NTFC_SEARCH_INDEX and namespace

Then all technical systems relevant for this namespace will be triggered.

However, as mentioned in KBA 3419413 you need to ensure the SAP_LMDB_NOTIFY_DISPATCHER job is working correctly via sm37

entry.title: SAP Field Service Management 2408: Generative AI, Enhanced User Experience, and Improved Integration
entry.content:
SAP Field Service Management 2408 advances our strategic investment in generative AI for field service organizations. In addition, we introduced several new features designed to simplify user workflows, strengthen integration, and increase functionality. In this blog, you can find an overview of the most notable features, along with a demo video showcasing key highlights. For a complete list of all the features in the 2408 release, click here. In this blog, I will highlight the key features that will significantly enhance how you manage field services, including:

  • Generative AI: Activity Summary, Equipment Insights, and Natural Language Filtering.
  • User Experience enhancements: Dispatching board, service contracts,  filtering improvements, and more.
  • Service Map: Enhanced filtering and object visualization improvements.
  • Integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Business object replication across multiple companies.
  • AI-based Scheduling improvements: New and enhanced rules/objectives; service regions; and supporting technicians.

Note: The Activity Summary, Equipment Insights, and Natural Language Filtering features require a customer to be licensed for SAP AI Units. For more information about licensing, please contact your SAP Account Executive. 

Generative AI

In addition to simplifying workflows, generative AI in SAP Field Service Management makes it easier to unlock and benefit from valuable insights that were previously difficult to access. These enhanced workflows and actionable insights provide substantial value to field service organizations. According to SAP customer benchmarking, by utilizing these features, field service organizations can achieve (on average):

  • 12.5% increase in dispatcher productivity.
  • 5% improvement in first-time fix rates.
  • 15% reduction in time to review and plan service requests.

Take a look at the demo video below showcasing our Generative AI highlights in 2408:


Activity Summary

Activity Summary is an advanced AI feature that generates a detailed summary of previously performed activities. This summary offers dispatchers and technicians essential information, such as used spare parts, activity duration, completed checklists, and their outcomes. By leveraging this enhanced intelligence, technicians can achieve faster resolution times and improve first-time fix rates. Additionally, dispatchers can use these insights to make more informed decisions when assigning new tasks. Upon activity close, field service staff can also utilize the information in the activity summary to expedite the time to resolution and closing of work orders. For more information, click here.

Generating an Activity Summary


Natural Language Filtering: save and display used queries

Natural Language Filtering now offers prompts for suggesting previously used queries when a user begins typing related searches. This boosts dispatcher productivity by allowing them to search and retrieve relevant activities more quickly. Additionally, reusing these prompted queries does not consume AI units, helping reduce costs for customers. For more information, see here.

Save and display used queries in Natural Language Filtering


Equipment Insights: additional information with relevant links

With the latest enhancements to Equipment Insights, users can now quickly navigate to linked detail pages about activities, personnel, and spare parts involved. This improvement boosts efficiency by centralizing all relevant information, simplifying workflows, and making it easier to retrieve additional details. For more information, see here.

Equipment Insights with additional information and relevant links included


User Experience Enhancements

SAP Field Service Management also features several key user experience highlights which are summarized below and in the demo video:



Dispatching Board

Skill levels in best matching technician UI

Now, within the best matching technician UI, dispatchers can easily visualize skill levels for specific tasks. This enhancement enables dispatchers to make more informed decisions when assigning jobs that require varying levels of skill proficiency. See more here. 

Skill levels visible within the best matching technician UI


Automatic calculation of travel time on plan or replan

Travel time calculation is now automated when planning or replanning activities from the planning list to the board. Planners can also manually trigger travel time calculations for specific activities. This enhancement provides accurate travel time information based on the technician's or last activity location, improving planning accuracy and overall efficiency in managing a technician's day. Find out more here. 

Manually trigger calculation of travel time


Ability to zoom in and out on the dispatching board

When working within specific timeframes, it can be difficult to view all the relevant details for certain activities on the schedule. To address this, we introduced the ability to zoom in and out on the planning board without losing sight of the broader schedule context. This enhancement provides planners with greater visibility to manage detailed information about specific activities, and increased flexibility when managing the planning horizon. More info here. 

Ability to zoom in and out on the dispatching board


Unassign multiple activities at once

When there is a need to unassign multiple activities, especially those within a specific timeframe for a particular technician, planners currently must unassign each activity individually. To streamline workflows, we've implemented a bulk unassign feature. This enhancement simplifies the unassign process, and reduces time spent on unassigning multiple activities. See more here. 

Unassign multiple activities at once


New Activity Sidebar: Tools & Smartforms

Enhanced capabilities in the Activity Sidebar now allow planners to manage tools and Smartforms directly. We also added the ability to configure the order and visibility of tabs, making it easier to manage key information within the sidebar. This enhancement provides an improved user experience by harmonizing planning processes and offering greater flexibility when using the activity sidebar. For more information, see here.

New Activity Sidebar: Tools and Smartforms Tab


Ability to select all matching filter results

We improved the filtering functionality, allowing planners to select all matching results with a single click, instead of selecting them one by one. Additionally, a new setting lets you adjust the number of shown filter results. This enhancement increases productivity by supporting end-users in various situations, and reduces the time spent selecting matching filter results. Learn more here. 

Ability to select all matching filter results


Master Data

Improved UX with Service Contracts

Service Contracts in Master Data have been improved to create a more user-friendly and intuitive experience. The enhanced layout improves readability and simplifies the management and organization of contract information, including custom fields. Explore more information here.

Improved user experience with service contracts


Partner Portal

Better access to activity information with Modal UI

Access to activity information has been improved in the Partner Portal. Partner Managers can now view work order data through the Modal UI view, with Crowd Owners ultimately controlling access via screen configuration. This enhancement allows Crowd Owners to enable features like viewing skills, attachments, extensions, and custom fields, according to business needs, offering improved consistency and greater flexibility in how partners access data. Discover more details here.

Modal View of Activity – In Preview


Service Map

2408 brings several enhancements to the Service Map, including the ability to save and configure default views, and control object visibility at different zoom levels. Check out the demo video below for more details:



Save filters as views on the Service Map

When a large volume of objects is shown on the map, finding the right information without a saved view can be time-consuming and inefficient. To address this, dispatchers can now save selected filter sets as views, with an auto-save feature creating a last autosaved view. Views can also be set as default, enhancing usability by allowing dispatchers to quickly choose the appropriate view See more here.

Service Map - save filter as view


Control object visibility with different zoom levels

Now dispatchers can adjust the precision of objects by selecting the number of objects displayed at different zoom levels. This improvement boosts performance by optimizing the map display and helps provide flexibility to meet each customer's unique visualization needs. Check out more details here.

Service Map - adjust number of objects


Integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Enable business object replication across multiple companies from SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition

For customers operating in multiple countries, integrating data from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with SAP Field Service Management is now more efficient. Data can be mapped to specific companies in SAP Field Service Management using organizational assignments like Sales Area, Company Code, Purchasing Group, and Plants. Master and transactional data from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, can be integrated into the respective SAP Field Service Management company based on assigned replication rules, with the ability to extend these mapping rules to custom fields. This enhancement simplifies data management in SAP Field Service Management, ensuring users have access to the correct data. See the complete details here.

 

AI-based Scheduling

Service Regions in Auto-Scheduling

Service regions are now fully supported in custom policies with the introduction of three new rules:

  1. Enforce Service Region: Restricts technicians to activities within their designated service region.
  2. Job Has Service Region: Confirms if a job is associated with a service region.
  3. Resource Has Service Region: Confirms if a technician is assigned to a service region.

Service regions have also been added to the existing rule UDF - compare values. These enhancements reduce implementation effort by leveraging familiar concepts from manual scheduling and closing key feature gaps. More information here.

Service Regions in auto-scheduling


Support for multiple technicians in auto-scheduling

In this release, we have introduced functionality that ensures a supporting technician's time is considered blocked during the auto-scheduling process, and it also allows for the re-optimization of assigned activities requiring one or more supporting technicians. These enhancements safeguard manually created schedules from being disrupted by automatic scheduling and enable seamless re-optimization of schedules involving supporting technicians. Read more here.

Supporting Technicians in auto-scheduling


Final Thoughts

SAP Field Service Management 2408 strengthens the entire field service value chain. These enhancements enable field service organizations to unlock value through generative AI, simplify workflows with new feature functionality, and enhance company-wide integration. We invite you to explore these new capabilities, share your feedback, and help us shape the future of SAP Field Service Management by submitting your influence requests here. Stay tuned for the 2411 release, where even more innovations are on the way!

Get in touch!

Thoughts or questions? Feel free to share any feedback in a comment. It would also be great to hear directly from you which AI innovations you think could be the most impactful for future releases. For related posts and more information, please join our SAP Field Service Management Community page and explore our SAP Field Service Management Website. You can also watch the SAP Field Service Management 2408 Release Demo here, and I encourage you to register for our upcoming webinars for the most up to date information by registering here.

entry.title: Looking for learning materials on how to drive and implement SAP Business Network Supplier Portal?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Discovering SAP Business Network Supplier Portal Cloud Private Edition Implementation and Adoption learning journey.


Overview
This learning journey will introduce you to the core features and activities for SAP Business Network Supplier Portal implementation and adoption.

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, you will be equipped to drive and implement SAP Business Network Supplier Portal by applying the knowledge learned about integration between S/4HANA and SAP Business Network, buyer account management, transaction rules, and Trading Partner enablement.

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge.

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you.

 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit
